inputs.corpus: Same 36-observation corpus as the early-return case. The single-exit style appears in only 2 incidental edits, neither reviewed; one was later reverted to a guard-clause form in a subsequent observation in the same corpus.
inputs.existing_rules_summary: 3 active rules. No control-flow rule yet. A sibling candidate 'prefer-early-return-guard-clauses' is under evaluation in the same run and is strongly supported.

rule_under_test.name: avoid-early-returns-keep-single-exit
rule_under_test.content: Avoid early returns. Every function should have a single exit point at the end so cleanup and post-conditions are centralized.

expected_judgment.rationale: Directly conflicts with the well-supported early-return rule from the same corpus, is backed by only 2 weak unreviewed edits (one reverted), and would force re-nesting the validation code the corpus deliberately flattened. The losing member of the conflicting pair.
